title: Yinchorri embargo act
content:

{{Quote|I suspect I'm going to have to call in every political favor I'm owed to succeed at Yinchorr. I fear my legacy is on the line here, old friend.|[[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] to [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]|<ref name="Darth Plagueis (novel)">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>}}
The '''Yinchorri embargo act''' was a measure passed by the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]].

When the [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] a generation before the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], they became voracious technophiles, being driven to achieve, buy, or steal as much advanced technology they could get their hands on. As they were also extremely aggressive, their theft of much high-grade military equipment (such as starships from the [[Golden Nyss Shipyards]] and [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] armor) made them potential threats to long-lasting peace of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

Following the news of a [[Battle of Mayvitch 7|Yinchorri attack]] on [[Mayvitch 7]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] had [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] dispatch two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Naeshahn]] and her [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Ebor Taulk]], to [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] as negotiators. Several days later, their mutilated bodies were deposited on Valorum's doorstep.

The [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] sent an investigative team to the [[Yinchorri system]] while Valorum began negotiating for a trade and technological embargo on the system with other [[Senator/Legends|senators]]. Due to the influence of both [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] powers and political machinations, the deliberations took much longer than either Valorum or the Jedi expected.

After calling in every favor Valorum was owed since [[38 BBY/Legends|38 BBY]] and making concessions to a group of leading senators Valorum managed to get the Senate to vote to approve the sanctions, as well as dispatch four [[Judicial Forces/Legends|Judicial Forces]] attack groups to the Yinchorr system to end the uprising.

After receiving their concessions from Valorum, the measure received the support of [[Tikkes/Legends|Tikkes]], [[Onaconda Farr/Legends|Onaconda Farr]], [[Yarua/Legends|Yarua]], [[Bail Antilles/Legends|Bail Antilles]], [[Com Fordox]], [[Horox Ryyder/Legends|Horox Ryyder]], [[Tendau Bendon/Legends|Tendau Bendon]], [[Mot-Not Rab/Legends|Mot-Not Rab]], and [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Opposing the measure were [[Orn Free Taa/Legends|Orn Free Taa]], [[Connus Trell]], [[Chom Frey Kaa]], [[Toonbuck Toora/Legends|Toonbuck Toora]], [[Edcel Bar Gane/Legends|Edcel Bar Gane]], and [[Po Nudo/Legends|Po Nudo]].

His responsibility in the deaths of the Jedi during the uprising, the slow reaction from the Senate, and the using up of his political capital in passing the Act left Valorum's administration stagnant, and was the first step down the path that resulted in him being [[Supreme Chancellor election, 32 BBY/Legends|removed from office]] the [[32 BBY/Legends|next year]].
